howItWorks.intro: This paycheck calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Pay = Gross - Federal Tax - State Tax - FICA
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How is take-home pay calculated?
faq.answer: Gross pay minus federal income tax, state income tax, and FICA (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45% = 7.65%). This does not include benefits deductions.

faq.question: What is the effective tax rate?
faq.answer: Your effective rate is lower than your marginal bracket because only income within each bracket is taxed at that rate. A $65K salary has an effective federal rate around 13-16%.

faq.question: What about pre-tax deductions?
faq.answer: 401(k) contributions, HSA, and health insurance premiums reduce your taxable income. Add these for a more accurate estimate.

faq.question: How often should I get paid?
faq.answer: Common schedules: weekly (52), biweekly (26), semi-monthly (24), monthly (12). Biweekly is most common in the US.

howWeCalculate.intro: This paycheck calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This paycheck calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When planning major financial decisions like buying a home, investing, or taking on debt
When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
When teaching or learning about personal finance and compound growth
